questions.text: How is Surah Ya-Sin often described in Prophetic traditions?
questions.answer: It is referred to as the 'Heart of the Quran' (Qalb al-Quran).

questions.text: What is the oath at the beginning of the Surah used to confirm?
questions.answer: That Muhammad (SAW) is indeed among the Messengers, on a straight path.

questions.text: What is the state of the disbelievers described in Verse 8-9?
questions.answer: They have shackles around their necks up to their chins, and barriers before and behind them, so they cannot see.

questions.text: What is written in the 'Imam-im-Mubin' (Clear Ledger)?
questions.answer: Everything: the deeds of people and the traces they leave behind.

questions.text: In the story of the town, how many Messengers were originally sent and how many was it reinforced to?
questions.answer: Two were sent, and they were reinforced with a third.

questions.text: Who came running from the farthest part of the city to support the Messengers?
questions.answer: A man (Habib al-Najjar) who urged his people to follow the Messengers.

questions.text: What was the man's reward for his faith after his people killed him?
questions.answer: He was told 'Enter Paradise,' and he wished his people knew how his Lord had forgiven him.

questions.text: How were the people of the city destroyed after rejecting the Messengers?
questions.answer: By a single 'Saykhah' (blast), after which they were extinguished.

questions.text: What is the 'Sign' regarding the night and day mentioned in Verse 37?
questions.answer: Allah strips the day from the night, and suddenly they are in darkness.

questions.text: What is said about the Sun and the Moon in their orbits?
questions.answer: The sun runs toward its stopping point, and the moon has been ordained phases until it returns like an old dried curved date stalk; each is swimming in an orbit.

questions.text: What was the 'laden ship' that carried the ancestors (Verse 41)?
questions.answer: The Ark of Nuh (AS).

questions.text: What will happen when the Trumpet is blown (the second time)?
questions.answer: They will emerge from the graves and race toward their Lord.

questions.text: What is the greeting from the Merciful Lord to the inhabitants of Paradise?
questions.answer: 'Salam' (Peace).

questions.text: What command will be given to the 'criminals' on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: 'Stand apart today, O criminals'.

questions.text: What will happen to the mouths, hands, and feet of people on the Last Day?
questions.answer: Allah will seal their mouths, and their hands will speak to Him, and their feet will testify to what they earned.

questions.text: Is the Prophet (SAW) a poet?
questions.answer: No; Verse 69 says 'We did not teach him poetry, nor is it befitting for him'.

questions.text: What parable is used for the revival of the dead at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: The One who created them the first time will revive them; He who produces fire for you from the green tree.

questions.text: How does Allah create something when He intends it?
questions.answer: He only says to it 'Kun' (Be), and it is.

questions.text: Who has the dominion (Malakut) of all things in His hand?
questions.answer: Allah, the Exalted.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of the Quran according to Verse 70?
questions.answer: To warn whoever is alive and that the word (decree) may be justified against the disbelievers.
